Fish Pulse
Fish Pulse v1.0 · Offline-first

Fish Pulse Premium

Everything in Free, plus:
Multi-device sync — start a record on your phone, review it on the office computer
Advanced statistics — species diversity indices, CPUE trends, solunar correlation
Bulk export — CSV, GeoJSON, PDF landing reports in one tap
Institutional accounts — share campaigns across your research team
Priority support & early access to new features

Vessel Track Recorder

Points
00:00
Duration
0.0
km approx
One GPS point every ~20 sec. You can move to other Fish Pulse screens while recording — it keeps running in the background of the app. The screen is kept awake automatically while tracking; locking the phone or switching to a different app still pauses recording (an OS limit no web app can override) — stop and save before doing either.

Log Haul

Haul Details

 
Gear & effort
Duration: —
Position optional
not captured
End-of-haul position — only needed for Mexican bitácoras that ask for start and end position separately.
not captured
Fauna de Acompañamiento (Mexico) optional
Only needed for Mexican shrimp/finfish trawl bitácoras — estimated kg of non-target species caught in this haul, kept or discarded.
Environmental Conditions (Mexico) optional
Only needed for Mexican longline/shark bitácoras.
Statistical Area & Set Details optional
Same number as the last set = same segment. Tap "New Segment" when you change gear or area — only needed for GARFO/Atlantic eVTR.
Protected Species Interaction optional
Only if this set interacted with a marine mammal, sea turtle, or seabird — leave blank otherwise.
Leave unchecked to return to the trip and log catch later — or combine all catch at end of trip.

Trip Summary & Costs

—
Est. value
Fuel
Fuel Consumed: —
Costs you can log now
Maintenance, permits, and port dues are usually added by the office afterward.
Crew On This Trip optional
Marking who was on board saves the office from retyping names at settlement — pay shares are still set there.
Trip Compliance Details optional ▾
Only needed if you plan to generate compliance-ready reports for a specific country. Fill in only what applies to you.
Before you close: closing locks new hauls and catch entries for this trip. The office can still add remaining costs and run settlement from the dashboard.

Vessels

Vessels & Captains

 
Add vessel
This decides which country-specific fields show up for this vessel — you can change it anytime from Vessels → Compliance.
All vessels
All captains
All crew
Species reporting codes
Match each species you log to the official codes used by fisheries authorities. Fill in only the codes relevant to where you report.
Vessel Compliance & Registration
These are optional and only needed if you plan to generate compliance-ready reports for a specific country. Fill in only what applies to you.
Mexico — CONAPESCA
The Aviso de Arribo applies automatically to any Mexican vessel. If you also fish a specific target species, select the matching Bitácora below — both will be available to export.
Gear setup below rarely changes trip to trip — fill it in once here instead of every bitácora.
Only needed for longline gear (e.g. shark) — leave blank if your vessel only uses nets.
United States — NOAA
European Union — e-Logbook
Sets the reporting mode automatically: 12m and over logs per haul (Block/Area, discards, protected species); under 12m uses a simplified end-of-trip declaration only.
Thailand — DOF / PIPO
Trawl gear setup below rarely changes trip to trip — fill it in once here instead of every haul. Reference minimums are from the DOF PIPO gear-inspection standard; they're shown as a guide, not enforced.
Species reporting codes
Match each species you log to the official codes used by fisheries authorities. Fill in only the codes relevant to where you report.
